last_packet.summary: Added a canonical held-out scenario manifest artifact and validation so external adapter plans can consume a first-class scenario-set contract and mismatched manifests block readiness.

verification.command: python3 -m py_compile flightrecorder/heldout_manifest.py flightrecorder/external_eval.py flightrecorder/cli.py flightrecorder/validation.py tests/test_heldout_manifest.py tests/test_external_eval_plan.py
python3 -m unittest tests.test_heldout_manifest tests.test_external_eval_plan tests.test_eval_summary tests.test_compare_rl_export tests.test_compare_gate tests.test_validation

risks: This packet provides the canonical held-out manifest and downstream blocking, not live external harness execution.
Single-source manifests can seed external adapter planning but do not allow cross-arm claims until another arm proves the identical scenario list.
The system /usr/bin/python3 is Python 3.9 and previously could not run the full suite because tests/test_deployment_hardening.py imports tomllib; /opt/homebrew/bin/python3 passed the full suite.

persistence.integration_blocker: main integration lock already exists after retries; main was not pushed and branch/PR remain open
